app.name: Roostvasum
app.tagline: Report location-based infrastructure damage with transparency.

metadata.title: Roostvasum
metadata.description: A geolocation-based infrastructure reporting platform for citizens, field officers, and administrators.

navigation.logoutConfirmDescription: Are you sure you want to log out of your account?

actions.unknownError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

home.title: Help the city move faster from report to repair.
home.description: Roostvasum helps citizens submit reports with photos, coordinates, and transparent progress updates.

features.locationDescription: Every report includes coordinates so verification and officer assignment can move faster.
features.evidenceDescription: Damage photos help administrators validate field conditions before assigning officers.
features.workflowDescription: Report status can be tracked from submission, verification, field work, review, to completion.

publicStats.title: Platform in Numbers
publicStats.description: Real-time statistics from reports submitted by citizens across all regions.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.description: From spotting damage to completed repair — four clear steps.
howItWorks.step1Description: Take a photo, pin the location, and submit your report in under 3 minutes.
howItWorks.step2Description: Administrators review your photo and coordinates, then verify or request corrections.
howItWorks.step3Description: A field officer is assigned and submits progress updates with photos until the work is done.
howItWorks.step4Description: Follow every status change on the public map — from report to completion.

categoryPreview.title: Report Categories
categoryPreview.description: Infrastructure issues are organized by category so the right team gets assigned.

mapPreview.title: Public Report Map
mapPreview.description: See verified infrastructure reports on an interactive map. Filter by status or category.

helpPage.title: Help Center
helpPage.subtitle: Find answers to frequently asked questions and learn how to use the platform.

categories.citizenDesc: Learn how to report damage and track repairs.
categories.adminDesc: Details on verifying reports and managing users.
categories.officerDesc: How to update progress and complete tasks.
categories.privacyDesc: Data protection and role access rules.

workflow.title: The Report Workflow
workflow.desc: Learn what happens to your report from submission to successful repair.
workflow.step1Desc: Citizen takes a photo, pins coordinates, and submits.
workflow.step2Desc: Admin reviews the photo and validates details.
workflow.step3Desc: A field officer is assigned to start repairs.
workflow.step4Desc: Repair is completed and updates are published.

contact.title: Still need help?
contact.desc: If you couldn't find the answer to your question, feel free to contact our support team.

faqs.q1: How do I submit an infrastructure report?
faqs.a1: You can submit a report by logging into your account, clicking the 'Create Report' button, uploading a clear photo of the damage, and pinning the location on the map.
faqs.q2: How can I check the progress of my report?
faqs.a2: You can check progress by typing your report code in the tracker box on the home page, or by clicking your report on the Public Map.
faqs.q3: What kinds of damage can be reported?
faqs.a3: You can report public infrastructure damage such as potholes, broken traffic signals, street light outages, damaged sidewalks, and public facility issues.
faqs.q4: Is my identity protected when I report?
faqs.a4: Yes, your profile details are masked. The public map and public report detail page only show the report code, category, photos, and general region.
faqs.a5: After an administrator verifies the validity of a report, the report status transitions to Verified and is assigned to the appropriate field officer based on the category and region.
faqs.q6: How long does a typical repair take?
faqs.a6: Resolution times vary depending on the severity of the damage and department availability. You can track real-time updates as the officer submits progress photos.

publicMap.title: Public Report Map
publicMap.description: Browse verified infrastructure reports across all regions.

publicReportDetail.timelineDescription: Public status changes only. Private notes and internal review data are hidden.

publicReportDetail.notFoundDescription: This report may be private, unavailable, or removed from public visibility.
publicReportDetail.privacyNote: Only public-safe report information is shown here. Reporter identity, private comments, officer identity, and audit data are hidden.
